guidebook.role.creator: By
guidebook.role.description.wathe:civilia: A mere bystander, the Civilian has arrived on the train unarmed. This role has absolutely nothing in its arsenal.
guidebook.role.description.wathe:vigilan: Armed with a gun, even upon first boarding the train. Their sole mission is to rid the %1$s of anyone with malicious intentions.
guidebook.role.description.wathe:killer:
A passenger with the intent of ridding the world of everyone who isn't like them. Their sole mission is to kill every non-killer player.

Maybe they have daddy issues.
guidebook.role.description.noellesroles::
A peaceful passenger blessed with otherworldly abilities created to aid them, yet cause them to stick out like a sore thumb.

Once every %2$s seconds, the Starstruck can activate their ability and gain both instinct and heavily faster movement speed for %3$s seconds, but the sudden transformation will emit power that gives their true role away.
Upon completing their current task, the Starstruck's ability cooldown will be decreased by %s seconds.
guidebook.role.description.starexpress:a:
The Allergic's medical record has proven that they have severe weaknesses- upon either eating or drinking, they may suffer severe side-effects.

The Allergic's allergy type is randomly chosen upon the modifier being assigned.

Upon consuming their affected consumable type, the Allergic will receive one of the following effects:
- Nothing (%5$s/%4$s chance)
- 3 seconds of Instinct (%6$s/%4$s chance)
- Immunity to the next attack (%7$s/%4$s chance)
- Immunity-bypassing Poison (%8$s/%4$s chance)
guidebook.role.description.noellesroles::
The Muzzler is yet another killer who happens to be armed to the teeth with duct tape.
I'm not even kidding.

For 75 coins, the Muzzler can purchase tape, and use said tape to silence one player.

If a silenced player remains outside for %9$s seconds, they will die due to lack of air flow.

Removing the tape from a silenced player can be done by clicking them %10$s times, but each time may take some mood from the silenced player- if their mood drops to 0 because of this, they may die to the pain inflicted upon them.

The Jester has come from a far-off kingdom, having expended their time as their king's servant. They now seek to play one final trick that will go down in history.

Upon being shot, the Jester will enter Psycho Mode (much like the ability Killers can purchase), causing bodies to hit the floor wherever they pass by.

Thankfully, other than that, they cannot inflict any damage themselves. Still, think before you shoot!

The Dreamer serves the killer organization, walking between reality and illusion.

Starts with Dream Imprint, which can be used on a target, allowing them to withstand one extra attack.

When a marked target takes damage, they are instantly teleported back to the Dreamer, and the Dreamer gains one count toward the number of non-killer players who have taken the Illusion Reagent.

The number of non-killer players required to take the Illusion Reagent is one-quarter of the total players. When enough non-killer players have taken it, the Dreamer's identity transforms into a killer.

To the Dreamer, reality is merely a dream that can be rewritten.

The Hacker is a technical elite working in the shadows to help killers achieve their winning goal.

The Hacker equips all killers with phones, enabling remote communication.

Cracking roles is also a skill a hacker should possess. After staring at a player long enough, they can crack that player's role, send it to all killers' phones, and get coins.

The Hacker can also use technology to help killers turn off lights, refresh weapon or ability cooldowns, and clear potion effects from them.

Remember, the Hacker needs to stay undercover. Never reveal your role to civilians too early...

The Cleaner is an cleanup expert sent by the killer organization.

Starts with Sulfuric Acid Barrel, which can be used to dissolve bodies, and can get coins when dissolve a body.

The Cleaner can use their ability to clear all dropped items on the ground.

For killers, the Cleaner is the most reliable partner — after all, no body means no evidence.

A born chaser, the Hunter thoroughly enjoys the feeling of prey being pursued!

When sprinting while holding a knife, the Hunter's movement speed is greatly increased. Hunting Knife will be added to the store — it has a longer holding time, but instantly cooldown when holding stops.

The Hunter can use his ability to instantly refresh the cooldown of all knives in his inventory, granting him terrifying chain-kill potential.

Running away only fuels his hunting desire!

The most popular person on the train, after all, who does not want a hot meal during the trip?

The pan is not just for cooking. In critical moments, it can stun enemies, and it can even block bullets.

Whenever a passenger eats food, the Cook can briefly see their location.

Remember, the Cook is still an ordinary person. Do not chase killers with a pan...

The most trustworthy person on the train, sworn to heal the wounded and save the dying.

The Physician can see poisoned or hallucinating passengers and has a longer sprint time to reach patients in emergencies. Reading the cause of death from bodies is also part of the job.

Starts with a Medical Kit, which can clear a player's abnormal status and grants a reward when used successfully.

Pills can be bought in the shop. Taking one lets the Physician withstand one blockable lethal attack.

The Robot has infiltrated this train; no one knows its purpose.

As a mechanical construct, it has no mood, is immune to poison, cannot be knocked out by Knockout Drug, and has unlimited stamina.

Every so often, the Robot can activate Night Vision mode, allowing it to see clearly in the dark.

Some say it's just malfunctioning, while others believe it carries some kind of mission...

The Drugmaker is the killer most skilled with poisons, savoring every second of his prey's suffering after being poisoned. He is only deployed to assist killers when there are enough players.

The Drugmaker can see players who are genuinely poisoned - after all, they are his masterpieces. Whenever a player is poisoned, he receives a reward of coins.

Poison Injector and Blowgun can be purchased at the shop - two distinctly different methods of poisoning.

For the Drugmaker, death is just a matter of prescription.

The Kidnapper doesn't kill openly; he prefers to turn his prey into obedient puppets.

Knockout Drug can be purchased at the shop. Using it renders a target unconscious, causing them to follow the Kidnapper. The Kidnapper also boards the train with Knockout Drug, just in case.

If the Kidnapper personally kills the dazed target, he will receive an additional reward.

A target affected by Knockout Drug will have a black screen, unable to attack, use items, or use voice chat - they can only follow blindly until the effect wears off. However, if you can get far enough away during this time, the Kidnapper won't be able to catch you.

No one knows where the Kidnapper takes these passengers, and no one wants to know.

The Executioner has boarded the %1$s awaiting a specific passenger's demise.

While they cannot inflict any damage themselves, they have access to a plethora of tools to toy with their victim.

As long as their victim does not fall to a passenger the Executioner may wish to work with, when the victim dies, the Executioner will become a random killer role.

Interestingly, the Executioner does not have Instinct, nor can they see who is a killer alongside them until their target is executed.

The Morphling is a killer who has mastered the art of imitation. They have the unique ability to disguise themselves to appear as any other living passenger on the %1$s.

If the passenger that they are disguised as dies, their disguise will be removed, and they will be revealed as their proper self.

Morphlings will often target passengers who cannot speak, as their voice does not change while they are disguised.

The Phantom has the ability to sneak around undetected- their ability allows them to turn invisible for a short period of time every 90 seconds.

While their body is undetectable, anything they hold is still visible to the naked eye.

Phantoms will often choose to break into rooms and wait inside them, ready for a victim they can kill while hidden out of sight.

The Swapper is a killer who may or may not have smuggled a modified portal gun onto the %1$s. They have the ability to swap any player with any other player, thus teleporting both players to the other's location.

While Swappers are more of a mentally disorienting role to deal with, they make a devious pair when alongside a Jester or another killer in Psycho Mode, as hiding from them is fairly difficult.

While not a killer immediately, Vultures hunger for flesh and bone, and once they are satisfied, another killer will arrive on the train.

Once the Vulture has "Consumed" enough bodies (by using their ability while looking at a body), they will become a random killer role.

Vultures can be seen by other killer roles as a cohort, but the Vulture cannot see who else is a killer.

When a Vulture consumes a carcass, they will be slowed down for just the tiniest moment, and a sound cue will play. Keep a keen ear out and you may be able to make the predator the prey!
guidebook.role.noellesroles:the_insane_d: The Insane Damned Paranoid Killer Of Doom Death Destruction And Waffles
guidebook.role.description.noellesroles:: uhm guys help the voices are telling me to jump off the train

The Conductor is the driver of the %1$s, and thus, they have access to every room on the train.

The Conductor holds a Master Key, which grants the ability to open every door on the %1$s. This item is dropped on death, and killers can pick the keys up too, so protect them at all costs!

If there are less than 10 passengers aboard the %1$s, the Conductor's Master Keys will appear as Lockpicks. Do not be fooled, your mind may play tricks on you. Hold the trigger.

The Bartender has been studying the art of drinks for a long time. They have a keen eye, and can see poisoned food/drink trays.

The Bartender also gains 50 coins per task completed. These coins can be used to purchase Defense Vials, which can be slipped into snacks or drinks to give the next drinker immunity to one attack.

Remember that killers can become immune too- make sure you trust whoever you give a drink to!

The Noisemaker was devoid of attention as a child.
Laugh at this attention seeker, everyone!

The Noisemaker's body will glow upon meeting their demise. They also gain 50 coins per task completed and 5 coins at passive intervals, which can be used to purchase Firecrackers.

The Recaller used to be a bank robber- but now they've gone good, and they're trying to lay low for a while. They've still got a knack for escaping dangerous situations, though.

The Recaller can set a location by using their ability, then teleport back to that location by using their ability again.
Teleporting costs 100 coins, and the Recaller gains 50 coins per task completed.

Recallers are one of the only roles that can counter most, if not all, dangerous situations. This is, however, limited by their reaction time.
The Coroner has the ability to read dead bodies like scrolls. They can figure out how and when passengers perished just by glimpsing their body, along with what role the passenger was.

While Voodoos are a profession often laughed at, this Voodoo in particular does have real abilities.

The Voodoo can pick one player to be brought down with them- if the Voodoo perishes, their selected player will die alongside them.

Voodoos can change their target once every 30 seconds.

The Mimic is a spy, pretending to be a killer alongside one of their friends (who may or may not be a convicted murderer on the run).

The Mimic appears as a Killer Cohort to other killers, but only has a fake knife.

If an alliance is forged with the Mimic, you could gain some high ground over any killers aboard the %1$s.

*blows up pancakes with MIND*



yeah this is just a civilian with a bunch of notes lol

If Vigilantes are so awesome, why haven't they made Vigilante 2 yet?

Spawns with a grenade instead of a gun.

I'd say "With great power comes great responsibility" or something corny like that, but honestly this role usually just bombs everyone around them as soon as the round starts.

Go kill some idiots for me.
-Circuitweaver <3

Your in-game height is set to reflect your real-life height.

Yeah, you're short now. Deal with it, sucker.

This passenger is somehow part Chameleon. How does Urbanshade keep coming up with this stuff??!

Standing still will slowly cause you to blend into your surroundings. Moving causes you to appear visible again.

The Guesser has the ability to pick any non-killer player and attempt to guess their role. If they guess correctly, the victim dies immediately.

This modifier can only be assigned to killers.

The Trapper has boarded the %1$s with a few mechanical tricks up their sleeve.

For 100 coins, the Trapper can purchase a Role Mine, which will find and note down the roles of the first three passengers to pass by its vicinity.

The Role Mine does not mark down names, only roles. The Trapper will still need to figure out who triggered the Role Mine and in what order.

Upon detecting a role, Role Mines will emit a quiet beep sound.

If placed in an area with more than three players already nearby, Role Mines will be instantly destroyed- but the Trapper involved will discover more than three roles.

The Graverobber has learnt from the Coroner, and has twisted their ability to assist their own murderous ideals.

The Graverobber can see information about dead bodies, much like the Coroner does.

This modifier can only be assigned to killers.

The Feather has mastered the art of aerodynamics and staying out of the way of others.

The Feather cannot collide with other players, and falls slowly.
These unique abilities allow the Feather to fall off the side of the train and still have time to save themselves.
guidebook.role.description.stupid_expres:
A Neutral role that has arrived on the train with the ability to carry on any one person's legacy, even after death.

While the Amnesiac begins each game as a true neutral role, if they come across a dead body, they have the choice to "Imitate" the body's role, thus becoming it themselves.
The Amnesiac can only do this once, as imitating a role removes the Amnesiac role from themselves.

The Arsonist has managed to smuggle both a Jerry Can and a Lighter onto the %1$s alongside themselves. Their only mission? Have some fun- at the expense of other passengers' lives.

The Arsonist can douse players in oil with their Jerry Can.
Upon dousing at least 30% of other passengers, they gain the ability to ignite the doused players, killing them instantly- regardless of their alignment.
guidebook.role.description.noellesroles::
The Necromancer has practiced the dark arts for years. They have gained the ability to bring the dead back and manipulate them to do their bidding.

Once every %13$s minutes, the Necromancer can interact with a body to bring that player back to life as a killer. The revived player's role is chosen randomly from all enabled killer roles, but it will not choose Necromancer again.

The Avaricious is essentially the Killer's extroverted cousin.
While their shop consists of the standard Killer products, the only way the Avaricious can gain coins is by being around other players when their "Payout" occurs.

Payouts are given every minute, and the Avaricious receives %12$s coins per eligible player within a %11$s-block radius.
guidebook.role.description.stupid_expres:
Lovers are linked with heart and soul. If one falls, the other will feel a disturbance in the force- and immediately follow suit.

Lovers always appear in pairs- either a Civilian-Civilian or Civilian-Killer bond is formed. It is allegedly impossible for two killers to fall for each other.

If this modifier is enabled, two players are always chosen to be Lovers.

The Initiate has boarded the %1$s with unsolved rivalries- and they come armed with the ability to end said rivalries forever.

The Initiate role is always chosen for two players. Both Initiates must race to earn 200 coins, then purchase a knife and be the direct cause of death for the other Initiate.
Upon completing this task, the surviving Initiate will become a random killer role.

If an Initiate dies from causes other than a direct kill from their opposing Initiate, the surviving Initiate will become either a random killer role, a random neutral role, or an Amnesiac. This behavior is configurable in the Stupid Express server-sided config.
